content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefina are playing a friendly tennis match. After the game ends, their final scores are revealed.

Your task is simple:


If both scores are equal, the match is a **Draw**.
If Chef has a higher score, **Chef wins**.
If Chefina has a higher score, **Chefina wins**.
For each test case, determine the result of the match.

Function DeclarationFunction NamedecideWinnerdecideWinnerdecideWinner – This function determines the result of a tennis game between Chef and Chefina based on their final scores.

Parameters
AAA : The final score of Chef.
BBB : The final score of Chefina.
Return Value
Returns "Draw""Draw""Draw" if both scores are equal.
Returns "Chef""Chef""Chef" if Chef has a higher score.
Returns "Chefina""Chefina""Chefina" if Chefina has a higher score.
Constraints
0≤A,B≤1000 \leq A, B \leq 1000≤A,B≤100
Scores are integers only.

Input Format

A single line contains two space-separated integers AAA and BBB representing the scores of Chef and Chefina respectively.


Output Format

"Draw""Draw""Draw" if both scores are equal, or
"Chef""Chef""Chef" if Chef wins, or
"Chefina""Chefina""Chefina" if Chefina wins.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers A and B — the final scores of Chef and Chefina respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the strings: "Draw" if A equals B, "Chef" if A > B, or "Chefina" if B > A.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case scores are equal → Draw. Second case Chef's score (15) > Chefina's (12) → Chef wins. Third case Chefina's score (20) > Chef's (8) → Chefina wins.
Both zero → Draw. In the second case Chef has 1000 while Chefina has 0, so Chef wins.
Compare each pair: 5<7 → Chefina, 7>5 → Chef, 9=9 → Draw, 0<3 → Chefina.

solution.approach: For each test case read the two integers A and B. Compare them: if A == B output "Draw"; else if A > B output "Chef"; otherwise output "Chefina". This requires only constant time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in O(1) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used besides the output buffer.
